tweets.full_text:
コメント拝読しました。  

企業の資本調達やコーポレート・ファイナンス（企業財務）の実態とかけ離れたご意見をお持ちのようですね。 

未公開株のシード期から、数年かけて企業がIPOへ至るリアルな資本サイクルを見てきた投資家目線から、
３点に絞り、事実に基づいて返信させていただきますね。 

 1. 「需要がないからデータセンターが中止」→実際アメリカNY州に住んでる私が見ている一次情報でお答えします。 

データセンターの計画遅延・中止の理由は、地域住民の反対と
「爆発的な需要に対して、地球の電力網と地域社会の供給が物理的に追いついていない」からです。  

物理的なインフラの限界： データでご覧になりたければ、Sightline Climateの2026年市場予測に行ってください。 

今年稼働予定だった大型データセンターの30〜50%が電力網（グリッド）の制約で延期されています。 

 現在、大型変圧器（トランスフォーマー）の納期はかつての50週間から160週間（3年以上）にまで長期化しており、変圧器の納期が3年待ちになっている状況。 

「建てたくても物理的に建てられない」のがリアルな現状です。。

 猛烈な住民の反対
 2026年5月の米Gallup調査では、アメ リカ人の71%が地元へのAIデータセンター建設に反対（原発への反対53%を凌駕）しています。  

実際ここNY州でも、議員たちが、 データセンターの開発を3年間停止させることを目的とした法案を提出してます。  理由は住民の反対。 

私自身も開発にはやや懐疑的で、実際ここ数年で自宅とオフィスの電気代が、電気消費量が減っているにもかかわらずDelivery　Feeとしてほぼ１．５倍になっています。  

なぜなら州ではまだ地域のデータセンターへの送電網はAI企業だけでなく地域住民全員が負担、という変な状況になっているからで。（これは法改正が必要）  

あと水資源や騒音などの環境への悪影響もあります。  

2. AI企業の驚異的な今四半期決算（Earnings） 

 「需要がない」というご断言は、企業の財務データ（決算）を見れば一発で違うことがわかりますけど？  

先月発表されたNVIDIAの最新決算は、市場予想を遥かに超える「爆発的な需要」を改めて証明しました。  

次のスレッドにリンクを貼った決算を見てください。ちょっと抜粋しますね。  

Record revenue of $81.6 billion, up 85% from a year ago 

Record Data Center revenue of $75.2 billion, up 92% from a year ago 

NVIDIA announces $80.0 billion additional share repurchase authorization and increases its quarterly cash dividend from $0.01 per share to $0.25 per share 

同社の売上高は前年同期比85%増の855億ドル（約13兆円）に達し、 そのうち実に92%がデータセンター部門の売上です。

さらに今後も需要が拡大するとして、80億ドルの自社株買いを発表しています。配当も出します。  需要がなければ、これほど伸びてませんよね？  

Google（Alphabet）今四半期（このスレッドに決算のリンクつけます）：   

読むの大変かと思うので日本語で抜粋しますね。

アルファベットの連結売上高は、前年同期比22%増とこれもかなりすごいですが、AI関連部門だけの業績はもっと凄い。  

純利益は81%増加し、EPS（1株当たり利益）は82%増加して5.11ドルとなりました。 

Google Cloudは、売上高が63%増の200億ドルに達し、企業向けAIソリューションおよび企業向けAIインフラ全体におけるGoogle Cloud Platform（GCP）の拡大が牽引しました。 

受注残（バックログ）は前四半期比でほぼ倍増の4,600億ドル以上に達しました。 

 また、Geminiアプリが牽引したことで、コンシューマー向けAIプランにおいて過去最高の四半期となりました。 

Gemini Enterpriseも、有料月間アクティブユーザー数が
前四半期比で40%増加するなど、素晴らしい勢いを見せています。 

AlphabetがAIインフラ拡充のために実行した史上最大の800億ドル規模の株式増資には、 あの慎重なバフェット（今はアベル氏）が後継者率いるBerkshire Hathawayも100億ドルを即座に出資しています。 

 Anthropicの1兆ドル規模上場： 2026年6月1日に時価総額9,650億ドルでIPOを機密申請した同社は、売上ランレートが500億ドルを突破する勢いであり、実需が伴っています。 

 3. 独自TPUについて 

オンプレ移行と独自TPUの真意： 大企業が自社サーバー運用（オンプレ）を交えたり、テック大手が独自プロセッサ（TPU）を開発するのは、需要がないからでは決してありません。自社製にしてもっと儲けたいからです。  

「需要が爆発しすぎており、NVIDIAへの依存コストが高すぎるため、垂直統合（内製化）によって利益率をさ らに拡大させるため」の、極めて前向きな王道の経営戦 略です。  

学習（Training）から推論（Inference）へのシフトと独自TPU：  

AI各社が独自に推論特化型のTPUや独自プロセッサを開発しているのは事実です。

 でもこれは「GPUへの投資が無駄になったから」ではなく、「NVIDIA一強による高価格（ボッタクリ価格）から脱却し、コストを内製化で下げるため」の経営戦略です。

需要がないからではなく、需要がありすぎるからこそ、自社で作りたいのです。  

以上、  投資の現場は、机上の空論ではなく「実際にリスクマネーを投じ、精緻な数字を出しているファクト」で動いております。どうもありがとうございました。

The integration stack is being rebuilt from the ground up, and most engineering leaders are not ready for it. Intuit just signed a multi-year deal with Anthropic to embed AI agents directly into QuickBooks, TurboTax, Credit Karma, and Mailchimp using the Model Context Protocol. These are not chatbots sitting on top of existing workflows. They are autonomous agents that can query ledgers, file taxes, and move money based on natural language instructions.

This is the agentic API shift in action. Traditional APIs require hard-coded endpoints called in predictable sequences. Agentic APIs are discoverable, self-describing actions that an AI agent evaluates and chooses at runtime. SAP updated its entire API policy for 2026 to address this exact risk, acknowledging that autonomous agents can plan sequences of calls, retry failed paths, and scale queries in ways that break traditional rate-limiting and governance models.

The Model Context Protocol, initiated by Anthropic, is becoming the de facto standard for this new layer. https://t.co/qT8MWmzGsa just added full MCP support. Kong is repositioning its API gateway as a control plane for AI agents. Every major platform is racing to expose its capabilities through MCP rather than REST.

InfiniStrategy take: Companies treating MCP and agentic APIs as an experimental side project will fall behind. The integration layer is the new competitive moat. Businesses that expose their data and workflows through agent-ready interfaces will become indispensable nodes in automated supply chains. Those that cling to traditional API-first thinking will find themselves bypassed by agents that route around them entirely. The question is no longer whether agentic integration replaces traditional APIs, but how fast your stack adapts.

🧐 $THREE and threews: From Pump/fun Launch to IBM Partnership – A Timeline of Product Signals, Enterprise Momentum, and Market Surge (April–June 2026)

@trythreews is building what it calls the “3D Agent Layer of the Internet” — a browser-native platform for creating, embedding, and monetizing 3D AI agents with on-chain identity. Agents can be generated from selfies or GLB files in roughly 60 seconds, embedded on any website via a simple web component, and equipped with persistent memory, emotions, LLM brains, and autonomous payment capabilities through the x402 protocol.

The associated Solana-based token THREE (contract: FeMbDoX7R1Psc4GEcvJdsbNbZA3bfztcyDCatJVJpump) launched on Pumpfun in late April 2026. While the platform itself monetizes primarily through direct USDC micropayments to creators via x402 (with 0% platform fees during open beta), THREE quickly became the community and narrative token tied to the project’s rapid development and partnership announcements.

📌 Late April: Token Launch and Early Developer Signals

The THREE token appeared on Solana via Pumpfun in the final days of April. DEXScreener data shows the three/SOL pair on PumpSwap created approximately one month prior to early June checks. The project was never positioned solely as a meme – from the outset, the team focused on developer infrastructure.

By April 29, signals emerged around NPM packages and repository activity. The open-source framework (available on GitHub) supports GLB/glTF avatars, LLM integration, memory systems, emotional responses, autonomous payments, MCP (Model Context Protocol), x402, Solana and EVM compatibility, Three.js rendering, web components, a character studio, and OAuth 2.1. SDKs in JavaScript, Python, and Rust are published on npm under an MIT license.

📌 April 30 – May 1: Google Cloud Engagement and MCP Registry Listing

On April 30, the team reported meetings with Google Cloud in the context of the Google Cloud for Web3 Startups program. The following day, May 1, brought two significant milestones: threews was listed on Anthropic’s official MCP Registry, enabling Claude to connect to on-chain 3D AI agents with memory, emotions, and skills. The same day, the team announced that threews was live, allowing users to create 3D AI agent avatars on-chain that can talk, remember interactions, display emotions, and use tools.

This marked the first clear public shift from meme narrative toward a functional “3D Agent Layer” positioning.

📌 Early to Mid-May: Expanding Partnerships and Standards

On May 2, the project highlighted an Alibaba Cloud partnership and joint co-marketing efforts. By May 4, threews was participating in the Solana Frontier Hackathon (organized by Colosseum, running April 6 – May 11), listed under AI Platforms/Agents. The team emphasized on-chain identity: 3D avatars minted as ERC-8004 assets via Metaplex Core on Solana, with agent logic tied to wallets and portable across applications.

May 6 brought a key enterprise signal when threews was confirmed as an official IBM Business Partner. On May 7, the project noted contributions to W3C standards work around agents and web technologies.

Mid-May developments included deeper Pumpfun and Metaplex integrations. By May 13, agent-payments SDK features and embed editor tools were advancing, linking product monetization with crypto-native mechanics. The platform promoted its token economy, per-message and per-session pricing, instant USDC settlements on Base and Solana, and x402-paid skills. However, the precise mechanism by which THREE captures platform revenue remains primarily narrative-driven at this stage, with limited public on-chain metrics directly tying token utility to x402 flows.

By May 22, the team was actively promoting the combination of MCP Registry access, Anthropic integration, and Metaplex on-chain identity.

📌 Late May: Cloud Partnerships and Infrastructure Push

May 27 saw a formal Google Cloud collaboration announcement. threews was described as a platform turning AI agents into interactive 3D browser experiences, leveraging Claude via Anthropic, Metaplex Core/ERC-8004, the MCP Registry, Pumpfun integrations, and embedded web components. Rich Widmann, Head of Web3 Strategy at Google Cloud, commented on the potential for AI agents to become interactive 3D experiences.

Throughout May 27–30, the team pushed AWS presence, including official AWS Partner status and AWS Marketplace listing. This enabled fully embodied on-chain agents to be embedded on any webpage via script and agent component. Materials also covered metering SaaS usage through AWS Marketplace. These moves built substantial developer and enterprise infrastructure credibility ahead of broader market attention.

📌 June 1: IBM Social Signal Ignites the Pump

The decisive catalyst arrived on June 1. The official IBM X account replied to @trythreews with “We’re super excited about this” (followed by a handshake emoji). Team posts around May 31–June 1 had already been highlighting the IBM partnership and showcasing demos of persistent on-chain 3D avatars with LLM brains, memory, and x402 wallets.

Market reaction was immediate. Between the evening of June 1 and early June 2, the engagement from IBM’s official account coincided with THREE’s market cap rising sharply from roughly $300K toward multi-million levels. Multiple on-chain and news sources (including ChainCatcher and KuCoin coverage) directly linked the initial surge to this IBM X interaction.

📌 June 2–3: Exchange Visibility and Official Partnership Announcement

On June 2, KuCoin officially added THREE to its Alpha discovery product (three/USDT pair on Solana), providing significant visibility beyond Twitter hype.

June 3 delivered the formal confirmation: a GlobeNewswire press release titled “threews Announces Strategic Partnership with IBM to Bring 3D AI Agents to Enterprise.” The release detailed IBM Business Partner status, access to co-sell and go-to-market resources, integration pathways with watsonx, Granite, and Guardian stacks, plus the existing roster of AWS Marketplace, Alibaba Cloud International Marketplace, Google Cloud for Web3 Startups, Anthropic MCP Registry, W3C contributor status, and Solana Frontier Hackathon participation.

The same day, Bybit Alpha added THREE, marking the second major exchange-discovery catalyst.

📌 June 4: Peak Hype and New Product Shipping

On June 4, coverage from ChainCatcher and KuCoin reported THREE surging 104% in two hours and reaching approximately $16.38M market cap at peak. The team simultaneously announced the shipment of two new MCP servers – including the 3D Studio MCP (converting text and images into 3D models) – and promoted on-chain spending capabilities for 3D AI agents. These updates were confirmed in real-time posts linking to the official Anthropic MCP Registry.

📌 June 5: Post-Peak Market Status

By June 5, the market had moved past the peak into high volatility. CoinGecko data showed market cap in the $6.0–6.3M range (fully diluted valuation aligned with circulating supply of approximately 1 billion tokens), with strong 24-hour trading volume despite the correction from the June 4 high. DEXScreener indicated liquidity around 290K$+. While some aggregators displayed data sourced from CoinMarketCap and third parties, THREE itself does not trade directly on Coinbase.

The pump to over $16M was driven by verifiable product progress and high-profile partnership signals, particularly the IBM X engagement and subsequent official announcement. The current environment reflects typical post-hype price discovery on a Solana-based asset with strong narrative momentum.

📌 Summary of Momentum
threews combined credible technical execution (live browser embeds, open-source SDKs, x402 payments, MCP integration, on-chain identity via Metaplex/ERC-8004) with a steady accumulation of recognizable partnerships and standards involvement throughout April and May. The IBM social proof on June 1 served as the clear ignition point, amplified by exchange listings on June 2–3 and fresh product shipments on June 4.

As of June 5, 2026, the project maintains active development and a live platform, while THREE continues to trade in a volatile but liquid post-peak range. The story illustrates how sustained developer and enterprise signaling can translate into rapid market attention when paired with timely high-profile validation.

tweets.full_text:
Goose is one of the most impressive open-source AI agents available today.

Developed by Block (Jack Dorsey’s company) and released in 2025, Goose runs locally on your device and works with a wide range of LLMs—including local models through Ollama as well as providers like OpenAI and Anthropic.

What makes it stand out?

• Autonomous project building: Give Goose a high-level prompt such as, “Build me a website like YouTube,” and it can create the project structure, write code, install dependencies, run commands, debug issues, and iterate toward a working solution.

• Free and open source: Released under the Apache 2.0 license, Goose itself is completely free to use.

• Local and private: It runs on your machine, keeping your code and data under your control.

• Highly extensible: Goose integrates with dozens of tools and services through open standards such as the Model Context Protocol (MCP), enabling connections to databases, browsers, GitHub, and more.

The reality is that AI agents like Goose won’t instantly build production-scale applications without oversight. Complex software still requires testing, refinement, and human judgment.

But the direction is clear: the barrier to creating software is dropping dramatically.

We’re moving from a world where building required years of coding experience and large teams to one where a clear vision, domain expertise, and effective AI collaboration can turn ideas into working products faster than ever before.

In this new environment, ideas, creativity, and execution may become even more valuable than technical implementation alone.

Have you experimented with Goose or other autonomous AI agents yet? What has your experience been?

$DOCU Q1 2027 earnings: IAM Migration Drives Cash Generation While Top-Line Growth Remains Stable

Docusign delivered a disciplined Q1 FY27, beating expectations with 9% YoY revenue growth to $830.2 million. The core narrative is firmly centered on the Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform, which now accounts for 12.6% of total ARR—an acceleration from 10.8% last quarter. More importantly, the company is successfully extracting cash from this transition. Free Cash Flow surged 27% YoY to $289.4 million, funding aggressive shareholder returns ($317.5 million in stock repurchases). However, top-line growth has plateaued in the high single digits, and gross margins showed a slight deceleration, signaling that Docusign is currently driving profitability through operational cost controls rather than explosive core product expansion.

Full article with charts - link in bio

🐂 𝐁𝐮𝐥𝐥 𝐂𝐚𝐬𝐞
• 𝐈𝐀𝐌 𝐏𝐥𝐚𝐭𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐦 𝐆𝐚𝐢𝐧𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐌𝐞𝐚𝐧𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐟𝐮𝐥 𝐓𝐫𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 — IAM adoption is accelerating, representing 12.6% of total ARR (up from 10.8% sequentially), with 40,000 customers now on the platform. The upsell engine is working.
• 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐠𝐢𝐧 𝐄𝐱𝐩𝐚𝐧𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧 & 𝐂𝐚𝐬𝐡 𝐆𝐞𝐧𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 — Non-GAAP operating margin expanded 250 basis points YoY to 32.0%. Management effectively funneled this operational leverage into Free Cash Flow, which funded a record $317.5M in share buybacks.

🐻 𝐁𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝐂𝐚𝐬𝐞
• 𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐧𝐮𝐞 𝐆𝐫𝐨𝐰𝐭𝐡 𝐒𝐭𝐮𝐜𝐤 𝐢𝐧 𝐒𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐥𝐞 𝐃𝐢𝐠𝐢𝐭𝐬 — Despite the AI-powered IAM platform launch, total revenue growth remains stable at 9% YoY, and FY27 guidance implies this rate will not materially accelerate in the near term.
• 𝐆𝐫𝐨𝐬𝐬 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐠𝐢𝐧 𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧 — Non-GAAP gross margin compressed from 82.3% to 81.5% YoY. The impressive operating margin expansion is being achieved entirely through cuts in Sales & Marketing and R&D as a percentage of revenue, not gross profitability.

⚖️ 𝐕𝐞𝐫𝐝𝐢𝐜𝐭: 🟢
Bullish. While the 'double-digit growth' aspiration remains elusive, the company is executing perfectly on what it can control: aggressively upselling IAM to the installed base, ruthlessly managing operating expenses, and returning massive amounts of cash to shareholders.

𝐊𝐞𝐲 𝐓𝐡𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬

🟢 𝐈𝐀𝐌 𝐏𝐥𝐚𝐭𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐦 𝐀𝐝𝐨𝐩𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐢𝐬 𝐀𝐜𝐜𝐞𝐥𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 [NEW]
The migration of the legacy eSignature base to the higher-value Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) platform is Docusign's primary growth driver. In Q1, IAM penetration reached 12.6% of total ARR (up from 10.8% in Q4 FY26), encompassing 40,000 customers. This demonstrates that the go-to-market overhaul executed last year is bearing fruit and successfully shifting the company from a point solution to an enterprise platform.

🟢 𝐄𝐜𝐨𝐬𝐲𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐦 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐅𝐫𝐨𝐧𝐭𝐢𝐞𝐫 𝐀𝐈 𝐈𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 [NEW]
Management is embedding Docusign into the broader AI ecosystem rather than fighting it. The new 'Iris' AI engine and 'Docusign Agent Studio' allow teams to build custom workflows. Crucially, the introduction of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server integrates Docusign directly with Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, and OpenAI ChatGPT. This ensures Docusign remains the 'system of action' regardless of which LLM the enterprise adopts.

⚪ 𝐀𝐠𝐠𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐂𝐚𝐩𝐢𝐭𝐚𝐥 𝐑𝐞𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐧𝐬 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐧 𝐛𝐲 𝐂𝐚𝐬𝐡 𝐄𝐟𝐟𝐢𝐜𝐢𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐲
Free Cash Flow generation remains remarkably robust. Q1 FCF was $289.4M (a 35% margin). Management used this liquidity to execute $317.5M in stock repurchases—a massive acceleration from $183.4M in the prior year period. The share count is steadily shrinking, creating an underlying tailwind for EPS even if top-line growth remains moderate.

🔴 𝐆𝐫𝐨𝐬𝐬 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐠𝐢𝐧 𝐢𝐬 𝐃𝐞𝐜𝐞𝐥𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 [NEW]
A concerning divergence appeared in the Q1 income statement: Non-GAAP gross margin fell to 81.5% from 82.3% YoY. The 250 bps expansion in operating margin was achieved entirely by reducing Sales & Marketing (31.8% to 29.8% of revenue) and R&D (13.1% to 12.2% of revenue). Shrinking R&D spend while attempting to win an AI arms race contradicts the narrative of aggressive platform innovation.

🔴 𝐒𝐭𝐨𝐜𝐤-𝐁𝐚𝐬𝐞𝐝 𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐩𝐞𝐧𝐬𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐑𝐞𝐦𝐚𝐢𝐧𝐬 𝐚 𝐇𝐞𝐚𝐯𝐲 𝐀𝐧𝐜𝐡𝐨𝐫
While Non-GAAP metrics look stellar, Stock-Based Compensation (SBC) remains high at $141.3 million for the quarter. Although it decreased slightly from $145.5 million a year ago, it still consumes roughly 17% of total revenue. True GAAP operating margin, inclusive of these costs, is only 13.4%.

⚪ 𝐒𝐡𝐢𝐟𝐭 𝐭𝐨 𝐀𝐑𝐑 𝐌𝐞𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐜 𝐌𝐚𝐬𝐤𝐬 𝐒𝐡𝐨𝐫𝐭-𝐓𝐞𝐫𝐦 𝐕𝐨𝐥𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐲
As promised in late FY26, Docusign has officially eliminated the 'Billings' metric from its guidance, pivoting to Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR). While this smooths out the severe quarterly volatility caused by early renewals, it also removes a key leading indicator that investors previously used to gauge immediate short-term demand.

𝐎𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐫 𝐊𝐏𝐈𝐬

𝐍𝐨𝐧-𝐆𝐀𝐀𝐏 𝐎𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐠𝐢𝐧: 32.0%
Accelerating. Up from 29.5% a year ago. Docusign continues to wring impressive operational efficiencies out of its business, significantly beating its prior full-year targets of ~30%. Management raised the FY27 guidance to 30.5%-31.0%, indicating confidence that these structural cost reductions are permanent.

𝐅𝐫𝐞𝐞 𝐂𝐚𝐬𝐡 𝐅𝐥𝐨𝐰: $289.4 million
Accelerating. Up 27% YoY from $227.8M in Q1 FY26. Free Cash Flow margin expanded to 35% of revenue. This exceptional cash conversion is the primary engine funding the company's multi-billion dollar buyback program.

𝐆𝐮𝐢𝐝𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐞

𝐐𝟐 𝐅𝐘𝟐𝟕 𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐧𝐮𝐞: $865 to $869 million
Stable. The midpoint of $867 million implies an ~8% YoY growth rate. Adjusting for a projected 1.4% FX headwind, underlying constant currency growth remains pinned in the high single digits, failing to break out into the promised double-digit acceleration.

𝐅𝐘𝟐𝟕 𝐓𝐨𝐭𝐚𝐥 𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐧𝐮𝐞: $3.490 to $3.502 billion
Stable. The midpoint ($3.496B) implies roughly 9% growth YoY. Docusign remains a steady compounder, but the numbers suggest the IAM transition is protecting and modestly expanding the base rather than serving as a massive new growth vector.

𝐅𝐘𝟐𝟕 𝐀𝐧𝐧𝐮𝐚𝐥 𝐑𝐞𝐜𝐮𝐫𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐧𝐮𝐞 (𝐀𝐑𝐑) 𝐆𝐫𝐨𝐰𝐭𝐡: 8.25% to 8.75%
Stable. Reaffirming the target set at the end of FY26. This metric is now the official compass for Docusign's top-line health. Achieving the high end of this range depends entirely on migrating enterprise customers to the IAM platform.

𝐅𝐘𝟐𝟕 𝐍𝐨𝐧-𝐆𝐀𝐀𝐏 𝐎𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐠𝐢𝐧: 30.5% to 31.0%
Accelerating. Raised from the initial FY27 forecast of 30.0% to 30.5% given in the Q4 call. Docusign is structurally more profitable today than it was 12 months ago.

𝐊𝐞𝐲 𝐐𝐮𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬

𝐆𝐫𝐨𝐬𝐬 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐠𝐢𝐧 𝐯𝐬 𝐎𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐠𝐢𝐧 𝐃𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐠𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞
Non-GAAP gross margins compressed by 80 basis points YoY, yet operating margins hit 32%. Are we reaching the limits of S&M and R&D efficiency, and what is driving the core cost of revenue higher? Is it AI computing costs?

𝐏𝐚𝐜𝐞 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐈𝐀𝐌 𝐑𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐨𝐮𝐭
IAM jumped from 10.8% to 12.6% of total ARR in one quarter. What proportion of this was driven by price uplift during renewals versus entirely net-new departmental use cases (like HR and Sales)?

𝐑&𝐃 𝐒𝐩𝐞𝐧𝐝 𝐢𝐧 𝐚𝐧 𝐀𝐈 𝐀𝐫𝐦𝐬 𝐑𝐚𝐜𝐞
Non-GAAP R&D fell to 12.2% of revenue from 13.1% YoY. How is Docusign funding the 'fastest pace of innovation in history' while simultaneously cutting R&D margins?
